transcript: [music] Looking for a car that's cheaper than you ever thought possible? You're in the right place. In this video, we're counting down 15 of the cheapest micro cars you won't believe exist in 2026. From futuristic electric pods and retroinspired bubble cars to ultra compact city commuters, these tiny vehicles are proving that affordable transportation can still be stylish, practical, and surprisingly fun. With rising fuel costs and growing interest in urban mobility, micro cars are becoming a smart alternative for everyday driving. Some are barely larger than a motorcycle, yet they offer weather protection, electric power, and incredible efficiency. Get ready to discover the world's most fascinating budget friendly microcars that are changing the way people move around modern cities [music] bubble car. The Microlino bubble car is one of the most eye-catching and affordable micro EVs on the market. Blending retro 1950s bubble car styling with modern electric mobility. Designed primarily for city driving, this tiny two-seater features a rear-mounted 12.5 kW electric motor, a top speed of 90 kmh, and offers up to 228 km of range depending on the battery pack. Its unique front opening door and compact 2.5 m length make parking almost effortless in crowded urban areas. Despite its small footprint, the cabin feels surprisingly practical for daily commuting. Pricing starts at around €19,990 for the standard model, making it one of the most distinctive and budget friendly microars available today. Hibau KP. The Hibau KP is one of the most affordable enclosed microcars designed for short city trips and daily urban commuting. Despite its compact size, it offers a surprisingly practical cabin with seating for two and a fully enclosed body for year round weather protection. The KP is powered by a 72V 105 aour lithium battery, delivers a top speed of 25 to 45 kmh, and can travel up to 100 km, 62 mi, on a single charge. Measuring just 2.5 m long, it's incredibly easy to maneuver and park in tight spaces. While official retail pricing varies by market, the Hibal KP is generally positioned in the $4,000 to $6,000 range, making it one of the cheapest micro cars available today. Eveta Prima. The Eveta Prima is a charming retro inpired microcar that brings the iconic bubble car design into the electric age. Built by Germany's electric brands, this compact two-seater is perfect for city driving. Combining classic styling with modern EV technology, it features a 20 kW electric motor, a 14 kWh battery pack, a top speed of 90 kmh, and an impressive driving range of up to 234 km on a single charge. At just 2.45 m long, it's incredibly easy to maneuver and park. With a starting price of around 16,000 to9,000, the Aveta Prima offers a fun, efficient, and surprisingly practical alternative to traditional city cars. A RI902. The ARI 902 is a surprisingly capable micro EV that proves affordable transportation doesn't have to feel basic. Built for urban driving, this compact two-seater combines tiny dimensions with practical everyday usability. It features a 15 kW electric motor, a top speed of 90 kmh, and a driving range of 110 to 250 km depending on the battery option. Measuring just under 3 m long, it slips easily into tight city parking spaces while keeping operating costs extremely low. Standard features include ABS, electric windows, and a reversing camera. With prices starting at around 13,990, the ARI 902 ranks among the cheapest and most practical electric microcars available today. F AW Bestun. The FAW Bestun is proof that electric mobility doesn't have to be expensive. This charming micro EV is designed for city life, offering a compact footprint, cute styling, and surprisingly practical four- seat accommodation. Powered by a 20 kW electric motor, the Xiaom reaches a top speed of 100 kmh and delivers a driving range of up to 170 km with newer versions extending that figure even further. At just 3 m long, it's perfect for navigating crowded urban streets and squeezing into tight parking spaces. With prices starting around $4,000 to $5,000, the Bestune Shia stands out as one of the cheapest electric microars available today, making affordable EV ownership a reality for many drivers. Smart Concept 2. The Smart Concept number two marks a return to Smart's microcar roots, reimagining the legendary two-seater city car for the electric era. This ultra compact EV measures just under 2.8 8 m long, making it ideal for crowded urban streets and tight parking spaces. Built on Smart's new electric compact architecture, it features a 35.7 kWh battery, an estimated 300 km of range, and fast charging from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes. Despite its tiny footprint, it promises a surprisingly refined driving experience. Expected to start at under €20,000 when production begins, the Smart Number two could become one of the most practical and affordable premium micro EVs on the market. PMV Electric EAS-E2. The PMV Electric EAS-E is one of the most affordable electric microars on the market. Designed specifically for crowded city streets and short daily commutes, this compact two-seater features a 10 kWh battery, a top speed of 70 kmh, and a driving range of up to 160 km on a single charge. Measuring just 2.9 m long, it's incredibly easy to maneuver and park while still offering practical features like air conditioning, a rear view camera, cruise control, and remote parking assist. With a starting price of around $5,500 to $6,000, the EAS-E delivers impressive value, making electric mobility accessible to budget conscious drivers. Freedom Evo. The Freedom Evo is an ultra compact electric microar designed to make urban mobility simple, affordable, and efficient. With its small footprint and modern styling, it's ideal for navigating crowded city streets and squeezing into tight parking spaces. The vehicle offers a smooth electric driving experience, a practical enclosed cabin, and low running costs that appeal to budgetconscious drivers. Depending on the version, it can reach speeds suitable for everyday city travel while maintaining impressive energy efficiency. Priced from around €10,990, the Freedom Evo stands out as one of the cheapest enclosed electric microars available, delivering a comfortable and eco-friendly alternative to traditional city transportation. Lydia Miley Max. The Ligier Millax is a premium microar that combines compact dimensions with features rarely seen in its class. Designed for urban mobility, this electric quadricycle offers a 12.4 4 kWh battery, a top speed of 45 kmph, and a driving range of up to 192 km on a single charge. Despite its small size, the Millie Max provides a modern cabin, generous cargo space, and advanced safety equipment, making it feel more like a conventional car than a typical micro vehicle. Measuring just over 3 m long, it's ideal for crowded city streets and easy parking. With prices starting around €17,000 to €19,000, the Ligier Millax delivers a refined and practical electric driving experience at a relatively affordable price. Dr. Berba. The DR Burba is a charming electric microar that delivers far more practicality than its compact size suggests. Measuring just 2.98 m long, this urban friendly EV is powered by a 14 kW electric motor paired with a 13.9 kWh battery, allowing it to reach a top speed of 90 kmh and travel up to 182 km on a single charge. What makes the Berber stand out is its surprisingly spacious interior and large cargo capacity for a vehicle in this class. Features like air conditioning, a rear view camera, parking sensors, and Bluetooth connectivity add everyday convenience. With prices starting at around €12,900, the DR Berba is one of the most affordable and practical electric microars available today. KG Motors Mibot. The KG Motors Miot is a fascinating Japanese microe built with one goal in mind, making electric transportation as affordable and efficient as possible. This ultra compact single-seater measures just 2.49 m long, making it even smaller than many key cars and ideal for crowded urban environments. Powered by a compact electric drivetrain, the Mibbot offers a top speed of around 60 kmh and a driving range of approximately 100 km on a single charge. Perfect for daily city errands. Its minimalist design keeps costs low while maximizing practicality. With a starting price of about 1 million yen, the Miot ranks among the cheapest electric microcars available, proving that simple, efficient mobility can still be incredibly appealing. Citroen Ammy. The Citroen Ammy is one of the most recognizable and affordable electric microcars on the market. Designed specifically for hassle-free urban transportation. Measuring just 2.41 41 m long. This quirky two-seater is incredibly easy to park and maneuver through crowded city streets. It features a compact 6 kW electric motor, a 5.5 kWh battery, and a top speed of 45 kmh with a driving range of up to 75 km on a single charge. While it's not built for highways, it excels as a practical city commuter. Its minimalist design, low running costs, and userfriendly nature have made it a popular choice across Europe. With prices starting at around €7,990, the Ammy remains one of the cheapest electric microars available today. Tazari EV0ax. The Tasari EV0ax is a stylish Italian microar built specifically for urban life, combining compact dimensions with impressive efficiency. Measuring just 2.13 m long, it's small enough to park almost anywhere while still offering a comfortable enclosed cabin, the Zeramax is powered by an electric motor producing up to 15 kW, reaches a top speed of 80 kmh, and delivers a driving range of up to 216 km with the larger battery option. Features such as regenerative braking, multiple driving modes, ABS availability, and fast charging add everyday practicality. With a starting price of around €10,400, the Zeromax stands out as one of the most affordable and distinctive electric microars available today. Swappip. The Swapperzip is a compact electric microcar designed to make city commuting simple, affordable, and environmentally friendly. Built for urban environments, this tiny EV offers seating for two, an enclosed cabin, and the convenience of electric power with very low running costs. Depending on the configuration, it delivers a top speed of around 45 km per hour, 28 mph, and a driving range of up to 100 km (62 m) on a single charge, making it ideal for daily errands and short commutes. Its lightweight design helps maximize efficiency while keeping ownership costs low. With a price starting at approximately €8,000 to €10,000, the Swappa Zip stands out as one of the cheapest microars available, offering practical transportation without unnecessary complexity. Unity 1. The Uniti 1 is a futuristic electric microcar that was designed to redefine urban transportation with its lightweight construction and smart technology. This compact 3-seater features a 68 horsepower 50 kW electric motor, allowing it to accelerate from 0 to 80 kmh, 50 mph in just 9.9 seconds while reaching a top speed of 120 kmh, 75 mph. Depending on the battery option, it offers an impressive driving range of up to 300 km (186 mi) on a single charge. Weighing only around 600 kg, the Unity 1 focuses on efficiency without sacrificing practicality. Its minimalist Scandinavian design and advanced connectivity features make it feel far more premium than its size suggests. With an expected starting price of around €17,000 to 19,000, the Unidi 1 remains one of the most innovative and affordable micro EV concepts developed for modern city life. AXM ecam ecity pack is a practical electric microcar built for effortless urban commuting. Popular across Europe, this compact quadricycle combines a surprisingly spacious cabin with the convenience of all electric driving. It features an 8.15 kWh lithium ion battery, a top speed of 45 kmh, 28 mph, and a driving range of up to 75 km, 47 m on a single charge. Measuring just under 3 m long, the ecity pack is easy to maneuver through crowded streets and squeeze into tight parking spaces. Despite its small size, it offers modern comforts such as a digital display, heating, and ample storage. With prices starting at around €13,000 to €15,000, the AXM eack delivers an efficient, user-friendly, and affordable solution for everyday city travel.

[music] If you think trucks are just big machines hauling cargo from one place to another, think again. The trucking industry is being transformed by groundbreaking innovations that are smarter, safer, and more advanced than ever before. From autonomous driving systems and AI powered virtual drivers to massive heavy hall transporters, smart cargo monitoring cameras and next generation safety technologies. These inventions are redefining what modern trucking can do. In today's video, we're counting down 18 next level truck inventions that are pushing the boundaries of engineering and making transportation more efficient than ever. Some of these technologies look like they're straight out of a science fiction movie, while others are already changing the way freight moves around the world. So, buckle up and get ready to discover the incredible machines, gadgets, and innovations shaping the future of trucking. Let's get started. Tatraforce automated driving. The Tatra Force automated driving system is a fascinating glimpse into the future of heavyduty transportation. Built on Tatra's legendary Force platform, this next level truck combines the brand's rugged off-road capability with advanced autonomous driving technology developed in partnership with mobility researchers and industry experts. The truck features Tetra's signature backbone chassis, independent suspension, multiple drivetrain configurations from 4x4 to 16x6, and engine options producing up to 690 horsepower. While the automated driving version remains in the testing and development phase, similar Tetra Force models can range from approximately $250,000 to well over $1 million depending on configuration and specialized equipment. NBOM Euro PX3. The Nboom Euro PX3 is a remarkable heavyhole innovation designed to move some of the largest and heaviest loads on the road with impressive efficiency. Built with a low- deck hydraulic platform, the Euro PX3 offers exceptional stability, a low loading height, and modular axle configurations that can be customized for different transport tasks. Depending on the setup, payload capacities can exceed 100 tons, making it ideal for construction equipment, transformers, and industrial machinery. Pricing varies significantly based on configuration, but fully equipped models can range from approximately $250,000 to well over $1 million. Its advanced steering system and engineering make oversized transport safer, smoother, and more [music] efficient. Ledge Alert Dock Safety System. The ledge alert dock safety system is a simple yet highly effective innovation designed to prevent one of the most common loading dock accidents. Drivers pulling away before loading operations are complete. Using bright visual warning indicators and intelligent dock edge monitoring, the system helps improve communication between warehouse staff and truck operators, reducing the risk of costly accidents, injuries, and cargo damage. Built for busy logistics facilities, it integrates easily into existing dock environments and operates with minimal maintenance. Pricing varies depending on installation requirements and facility size, but complete systems typically start at several thousand, making safety a worthwhile investment. Famonville Multimax. The Famonville Multimax is a standout heavyhole transport solution engineered for moving oversized construction machinery, industrial equipment, and other massive loads with remarkable efficiency. What makes it a next level truck invention is its modular low-loadader design, which combines a lightweight chassis with impressive payload capacity and exceptional maneuverability. Depending on the configuration, the Multimax can feature two to 10 axles, hydraulic suspension, and steering systems that improve stability on challenging routes. Prices vary widely based on axle count and customization, but most units range from approximately $150,000 to over $700,000. For heavy transport professionals, it's a powerful blend of flexibility, durability, and smart engineering. Tire Pro LB. The Tireax Pro LB is a clever trucking innovation that helps fleets reduce tire wear, improve fuel efficiency, and enhance overall safety. Developed by Hendrickson, this automatic tire inflation system continuously monitors and maintains proper tire pressure while the trailer is in motion, eliminating the need for frequent manual checks. The system is designed for linehole and long-d distanceance transport applications and can significantly extend tire life while reducing downtime. Depending on trailer specifications and installation requirements, the Tires Pro LB typically costs several thousand per trailer, making it a smart investment that can quickly pay for itself through lower maintenance and operating costs. Garmin Diesel Dual Blind Spot camera. The Garmin DEL dual view blind spot camera is a smart safety innovation designed specifically for truck drivers who need better visibility around large vehicles. This next level gadget provides a live video feed of blind spots directly to a compatible Garmin Dazzle Navigator, helping drivers spot vehicles, cyclists, and obstacles that might otherwise go unnoticed. The compact camera offers a wide viewing angle, weather resistant construction, and wireless connectivity for easy installation. Typically priced between $250 and $400 depending on the package, the dual view system delivers an extra layer of confidence and safety that can make every mile on the road less stressful and more secure. Turberg RT43. The Turberg RT43 is a purpose-built terminal tractor that showcases how modern engineering can transform port logistics and industrial operations. Designed for moving trailers quickly and efficiently within distribution centers and container terminals, this powerful machine combines durability with operator comfort and advanced safety features. The RT43 is typically equipped with a Cumins diesel engine producing up to 250 horsepower, a 4x2 drivetrain, and a lifting capacity of around 36 tons. Depending on specifications, options, and market conditions, prices generally range from approximately $120,000 to over $200,000. Its reliability, maneuverability, and productivity make it a standout innovation in the world of heavy transport equipment. Stonom cargo space monitoring camera. The StonCam cargo space monitoring camera is [clears throat] a smart logistics innovation designed to give drivers and fleet operators a clear view of what's happening inside a trailer or cargo compartment in real time using highdeinition cameras, infrared night vision, and rugged weatherresistant construction. The system helps prevent cargo theft, loading errors, and damage during transport. Video feeds can be displayed directly in the cab, allowing drivers to monitor valuable loads without stopping. Depending on camera configuration, recording capabilities, and installation requirements, prices typically range from around $300 to over $1,500. It's a practical technology that adds both security and peace of mind to modern freight operations. Feonville Flexax. The Feonville Flexax is an impressive heavy transport innovation built to handle oversized and high value cargo with exceptional flexibility. Designed as a modular low-loadader trailer, it features hydraulically steered axles, a low-loading deck, and adaptable configurations [music] that allow operators to transport everything from construction equipment to massive industrial components. Depending on the setup, the Flex Max can support payloads exceeding 70 tons while maintaining excellent maneuverability on tight routes. Prices vary based on axle count and customization, typically ranging from around $200,000 to more than $800,000. Its combination of versatility, strength, and advanced engineering makes it a true next level transport solution. Weson BDF removable system. The WSOB WCON BDF removable system is an innovative logistics solution designed to make cargo transport faster, more flexible, and far more efficient. Built around the popular BDF swap body concept, the system allows truck bodies and containers to be quickly loaded, unloaded, or exchanged without the need for heavy lifting equipment. Its hydraulic lifting and locking technology significantly reduces turnaround times while improving operational productivity. Depending on configuration and vehicle integration, prices can range from approximately $20,000 to over $80,000. For transport companies handling diverse cargo operations, the Wickon BDF system is a smart investment that streamlines logistics and maximizes vehicle utilization. Pavelli helicopter transporter. The Pavelli helicopter transporter is a highly specialized heavy hall solution designed for safely transporting helicopters by road without the need for complete disassembly. This next level transport invention combines a low-loading platform, custom helicopter support structures, and advanced securing systems that protect valuable aircraft during transit. Engineered for flexibility and precision, it allows operators to move helicopters quickly between maintenance facilities, airfields, and deployment locations. Depending on size, configuration, and customization, prices can range from approximately $250,000 to well over $1 million. Its unique design, operational efficiency, and ability to handle high value cargo make it an impressive innovation in the world of specialized transport equipment. Wabi Virtual Driver AI. The Wabi Virtual Driver AI is one of the most exciting innovations in the future of trucking, combining advanced artificial intelligence with autonomous driving technology to create a fully digital truck driver. Developed by Wabby, the system uses a combination of cameras, radar, LAR sensors, and powerful onboard computing to perceive its surroundings and make driving decisions in real time. What makes it especially impressive is its AI first architecture and simulation platform which allow rapid training and validation before realworld deployment. While Wabby does not publicly disclose pricing, [music] industry estimates suggest autonomous driving systems can add hundreds of thousands of dollars to a truck. As a next level truck invention, the Wabby driver offers a fascinating glimpse into the future of freight transportation. Echiri band-up system. The Aeri bandup system is a clever cargo handling innovation that makes loading and unloading trailers faster, safer, and more efficient. Integrated into Aeri's side opening trailer design, the system uses lifting straps and a mechanical assistance mechanism to raise or secure side curtains and cargo coverings with minimal effort. This reduces manual labor, improves workplace safety, and speeds up operations in busy logistics environments. Built for demanding commercial use, the system is especially useful when handling palletized and oversized freight. While pricing varies by trailer specification and customization, Bandup equipped trailers can cost significantly more than standard models, often exceeding $80,000. Its practicality and time-saving design make it a standout advancement in modern freight transport. Mayan TGX. The Mayan TGX is a flagship long haul truck that combines power, efficiency, and cuttingedge technology into one impressive package. As a next level trucking innovation, it features advanced driver assistance systems, aerodynamic design, and a highly digitalized cockpit that helps reduce driver fatigue on long journeys. Depending on the model, the TGX is equipped with man's D26 or D38 diesel engines, producing between $430 and 640 horsepower, paired with the efficient man Tipmatic transmission. Prices typically start around $130,000 and can exceed $220,000 for premium configurations. With its blend of comfort, fuel efficiency, and intelligent safety features, the man TGX remains one of the most advanced trucks on the road today. DAFF ADAS. The DAFF advanced driver assistance systems package is a standout innovation that brings cuttingedge safety technology to modern trucking. Designed to reduce accidents and driver fatigue, the system combines features such as adaptive cruise control, lane departure warning, emergency braking, traffic sign recognition, and advanced blind spot monitoring. Integrated into DF's latest XF, XG, and XG Plus trucks, ADAS continuously monitors the road and assists drivers in challenging situations. While the technology is typically included within premium truck packages rather than sold separately, a fully equipped DF truck can range from approximately $140,000 to over $250,000. With its intelligent safety features and real-time assistance, DAFF ADUs represents a major step forward in commercial vehicle technology and road safety. Side dump industry side dump. The side dump industry's side dump trailer is a game-changing innovation in the world of bulk material transport. Unlike traditional dump trailers, it unloads material by tipping to the side, providing exceptional stability and significantly reducing the risk of rollovers. Designed for hauling aggregates, soil, mining materials, and construction debris, it features a rugged, high strength steel frame, large capacity tub design, and a powerful hydraulic dumping system. Depending on the model, payload capacities can exceed 40 tons. Pricing typically ranges from approximately $80,000 to over $200,000. With its fast unloading capability, improved safety, and impressive productivity, the side dump trailer has become a favorite solution for demanding construction and mining operations. Markin covering. The Markin covering system is a smart transport innovation designed to make loading operations safer, faster, and more efficient. Widely used on dump trailers, tippers, and bulk hall vehicles, the system automates the process of covering and uncovering cargo with durable tops, helping protect loads from weather, dust, and material loss during transit. Depending on the model, it can feature electric or hydraulic operation, lightweight aluminum components, and remote control functionality for added convenience. Prices typically range from around $3,000 to over $15,000 depending on vehicle size and configuration. Its ability to improve safety, reduce manual effort, and ensure regulatory compliance makes it a valuable upgrade for modern transport fleets.

These aren't your typical RVs or full-size camper vans. They're K campers, tiny, clever, and incredibly efficient machines built on Japan's famous K class vehicles. Originally designed after World War II to provide affordable mobility in a country with tight streets and limited space, these compact vehicles have evolved into some of the most creative mini campers on the planet. What looks small on the outside often hides a surprisingly smart living space inside. Foldable beds, pop-up roofs, mini kitchens, storage tricks, and even off-grid power setups packed into vehicles barely bigger than city cars. In this video, we're counting down 15 Japanese K campers you need to see. Each one proving that you don't need a massive RV to travel in comfort and style. From factory-built conversions to wild DIY builds, these mini camper vans are redefining what life on the road really means. Let's jump right in. >> [music] >> Field Life Vida. The Field Life Vida is one of the most impressive Japanese K campers, proving that a compact van can still deliver a comfortable camping experience. Built on the reliable Daihatsu Atrai platform, it features a clever pop-up roof that expands interior headroom to approximately 2,000 mm, creating a surprisingly spacious living area. Inside, a large sofa quickly converts into an adjustable bed, while underfloor compartments, overhead cabinets, and a dedicated shoe storage area maximize organization. The Vida also includes a round sink, pull-out shower faucet, and a versatile table for indoor or outdoor use. Power comes from a sub battery system with an optional lithium ion upgrade for extended adventures. Despite offering generous interior space and practical amenities, the camper remains under 2 m tall when closed, making it easy to drive and park daily. Priced at around $25,000 for the four-wheel drive version, the Field Life Vida is a smart blend of practicality, comfort, and everyday usability. MYS Mystic Mini Pop Bee. The MYS Mystic Mini Pop Bee is one of Japan's most beloved K campers, built on the Toyota Pixis truck and refined through more than a decade of development. The 2024 model introduces several upgrades while maintaining the charm that made it popular among camping enthusiasts. One of its biggest strengths is customization, allowing buyers to personalize the exterior paint, interior finishes, and additional features to suit their needs. Inside, the camper offers a warm, cozy atmosphere with subtle vintage styling complemented by redesigned cabinetry featuring wooden handles and improved storage solutions. Amenities include a 12-V cooler, a bottleneck water meter, and the brand's signature denim colored cushions and upholstery that create a modern, yet inviting feel. Compact and easy to drive, it's perfect for weekend getaways and light camping adventures, though it's not designed for rough off-road travel. Priced at around $25,000, the Mini Pop Bee delivers a unique blend of personality, comfort, and practicality. White House Campers N-Van Compo. The White House Campers N-Van Compo transforms the Honda N-Van into a remarkably capable Japanese K camper designed for both hobbyists and overnight adventurers. Often described as a mobile secret base, it makes excellent use of every inch of space with a flexible interior that can be arranged for sleeping, relaxing, or gear storage. The cushioned bed mattress provides a comfortable sleeping area, while the raised bed board creates up to 1,800 mm of standing room. Features include a sink with indoor and outdoor access, microwave oven, fold-down table, water tanks, power outlets, and a removable mosquito net. An FF type air heater keeps the cabin warm without running the engine, while the exclusive rotating driver seat adds extra versatility. Outside, an optional side awning creates a terrace-like living space, and a rear-mounted bike rack can carry up to three bicycles. Buyers can also add a cargo rack for extra storage. With its practical layout, clever features, and adventure-ready design, the N-Van Compo offers an impressive camping experience for around $20,000. Honda N-Box Joy. The Honda N-Box Joy is a clever Japanese Kei camper designed [snorts] for leisure driving, weekend adventures, and even overnight camping. Built on the third generation N-Box platform, it combines compact dimensions with impressive practicality. The exterior stands out with a redesigned bumper, unique air intakes, retro-inspired wheels, and a bold black grill, while optional LED bumper lights, adventure decals, and a roof rack add an outdoor-ready look. Inside, the rear seats fold completely flat to create a comfortable sleeping area, and hidden cargo floor compartments provide useful storage. Optional awnings extend the living space outdoors, turning any stop into a cozy campsite. Power comes from a 658 cubic centimeter three-cylinder engine producing 58 horsepower, or an available turbocharged version with 63 horsepower. Both are paired with a CVT transmission and offered in either front-wheel or all-wheel drive configurations. Starting at around $12,000, the N-Box Joy delivers an excellent balance of city-friendly maneuverability, comfort, and camping versatility in a remarkably compact package. K-Works Exclusive Mini Bird. The K-Works Exclusive Mini Bird is a premium Japanese Kei camper built [snorts] on the Daihatsu Atrai. Designed for solo travelers and pet-friendly adventures, it focuses heavily on comfort and smart space usage, featuring a fully flat bed layout that converts the cabin into a cozy sleeping area with a pressure-dispersing mattress. Inside, it includes a compact galley with a sink, foldable table, and even a microwave for convenience on the road. One of its biggest highlights is the powerful electrical system, offering up to a 300 amp hour lithium battery option, a 2,000 watt inverter, and support for solar charging, making it highly capable for off-grid use. It also supports air conditioning operation for extended periods. Additional features include external water access, a 12-volt cooler option, and premium storage solutions. Despite its compact Kei size, it delivers a surprisingly luxurious, well-equipped camping experience at around $39,800. Nuts RV Spinach. The Nuts RV Spinach is a premium Japanese Kei camper that brings surprising space and comfort into a compact van body. Built on platforms like the Suzuki Every or Nissan Clipper, it focuses on maximizing usability through smart design. One of its standout features is the optional pop-up roof, which increases interior height and adds extra sleeping space for up to four people. Inside, it offers a full flat floor conversion with a proper dinette layout, allowing passengers to sit face-to-face comfortably. The interior uses lightweight PVC furniture, improving durability and reducing overall weight while maintaining a clean, modern finish. A hard urethane mattress system ensures solid sleeping comfort, and multiple layout options allow flexible bed or lounge configurations. The camper also supports optional sink installations, LED lighting, USB power ports, and storage cabinets for daily essentials. Priced from around $25,000 to $30,000, depending on configuration, the Spinach delivers a refined balance of practicality, comfort, and clever engineering in true K camper style. Vantech Lunetta PZ. The Vantech Lunetta PZ is a refined Japanese K camper that transforms the compact Suzuki Every into a surprisingly comfortable micro motorhome. Designed for practical travel, it retains a four-seat layout while offering a cleverly engineered interior that maximizes every inch of space. The rear seats and cargo floor fold flat to create a comfortable sleeping area for two, while a ceiling-mounted shelf and smart storage solutions keep gear organized. A compact sink doubles as a work surface, and a swing-out table provides a functional dining or workstation setup. Power access is supported through a 100-V outlet, alongside standard 12-V sockets, making it easy to run small appliances or charge devices on the go. Some configurations even allow optional TV installation for entertainment. With its tall roof advantage and smart interior packaging, the Lunetta PZ delivers a cozy hotel-like camping experience in a minimal footprint, typically priced in the mid-budget range for K conversions. Rakunero Lily. The Rakunero Lily is a well-designed Japanese K camper built on the Daihatsu Hijet Cargo, offering a smart balance between compact size and practical living space. It measures 3,395 by 1,475 by 1,890 mm and is powered by a 658 cubic centimeter inline 3DOHC turbo engine producing around 64 horsepower, paired with CVT and available in 2WD or 4WD. Inside, it accommodates four passengers and sleeps two comfortably thanks to a flat-folding bed system supported by a strong yet lightweight aluminum frame. Storage is cleverly integrated beneath the bed and around the cabin, making use of every inch. Optional features include an AGM 100 amp hour sub battery, 18 L compressor fridge, LED lighting, and DC/USB power outlets with optional insulation and charging systems for extended trips. Priced from around $19,000, it's an affordable versatile micro camper built for everyday use and weekend escapes. Oka Atrai SV. The Oka Atrai SV is a refined Japanese K camper based on the Daihatsu Atrai, designed for comfort, practicality, and efficient space use. It is powered by a 660 cubic centimeter turbocharged three-cylinder engine producing around 64 horsepower, paired with a CVT and available in 2WD or 4WD. Inside, it offers seating for four and sleeping space for two, built around a premium triple-layer urethane mattress system for enhanced comfort. The interior includes a compact kitchen setup with sink, fold-out table, and optional microwave along with smart storage throughout. A 100-V power system with multiple outlets and USB ports supports appliances backed by a sub battery and optional solar setup. Priced around $30,000, it delivers a balanced mix of luxury and compact camping capability. Honda N-Van E. The Honda N-Van E is a Japanese K-Camper based on the Honda N-Van platform designed for commercial and recreational use. It features a high-capacity battery compact e-axle and supports fast charging in about 30 minutes. A 1,500-W external power supply allows running appliances, tools, and camping gear, making it ideal for mobile cafes, workshops, and off-grid travel. The interior offers a flat cargo space that can be converted for sleeping or work using Honda access accessories like roof racks and mesh pockets. It includes an 8-in navigation system and rear camera assistance. Priced around $18,000, it is versatile. Field Life Balocco. The Field Life Balocco is a rugged yet compact Japanese K-Camper built on the Daihatsu Hijet truck platform designed for practical off-grid travel. It features a highly insulated Hydro Back body with double acrylic windows helping maintain a stable interior temperature in all seasons. Inside, it offers a convertible seating area that turns into a comfortable flat bed along with a small kitchenette including a sink, water system with 13-L tanks, and optional refrigerator. Smart storage, LED lighting, and a roof carrier improve usability and space efficiency. Optional upgrades include solar panels, FF heater, awnings, and enhanced electrical systems. With prices around $25,000 to $30,000, depending on configuration, the Beloco delivers a durable, self-sufficient camping experience in a compact key-size package. Chantier Roule Daihatsu Hijet The Chantier Roule Daihatsu Hijet is a budget-friendly DIY Japanese Kei camper that transforms a simple Daihatsu Hijet microvan into a compact living space. Known for its reliable Kei class platform and 660 cubic centimeter engine, the Hijet provides a solid base for customization. This build focuses on practicality using hand-crafted wooden panels to create a warm, cabin-like interior. Inside, it features a flat sleeping bed with storage underneath, modular shelving for cooking gear, spices, and daily essentials, plus a compact seating and storage layout. A portable power station supports off-grid use along with a small 11 L fridge. LED strip lighting, curtain partitions, hooks, and organizers enhance comfort and privacy. Even a portable toilet is included. It's an affordable, functional micro camper built for simple travel. Tin Can Suzuki Super Carry The Tin Can Suzuki Super Carry is a simple yet clever DIY Japanese Kei camper built from a bare-bones Suzuki Super Carry van that can cost around $2,000 in used condition. It focuses on budget-friendly conversion, turning cargo space into a compact living area designed for minimal comfort and practicality. Inside, it features a bed with storage underneath and simple fittings for essentials. It is mainly designed for weekend trips and overnight stays rather than full off-grid living. Its small footprint makes it easy to drive and park in tight city streets, a great budget camper example today. Youme No Caban Deni. The Youme No Caban Deni is a thoughtfully organized Japanese Kei camper built on the Suzuki Spacia platform, designed for long-term travel and minimalist living. Instead of heavy modification, it focuses on smart storage and everyday practicality. The rear area holds essentials like a Muji storage case, solar panel, sunshade, bedding, rice, and daily supplies, while the back seats store bags, clothing, power equipment, and water. Even the front cabin is efficiently used with eco bags, documents, and cleaning tools. Cooking gear, such as a clay pot and cassette stove, sits neatly under the passenger seat. A foldable mattress system converts the interior into a comfortable sleeping space. Based on a 4WD Spacia, costing around $19,000, it's a true example of intelligent DIY camper design.

America's wildest idea of the future is no longer parked in the imagination. It's lifting straight off the ground. From California test sites to New York skyline demos, a new generation of flying cars is turning science fiction into something surprisingly real. These aren't traditional vehicles with wings bolted on. They're electric vertical takeoff machines built to rise like helicopters and glide like aircraft, all powered by advanced battery systems and computer-controlled flight. Startups like Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, and others are racing to perfect designs that promise to cut hour-long traffic jams into minutes in the air. Some prototypes already carry passengers on short test routes while regulators work to approve full commercial use. It still feels futuristic, but the momentum is undeniable. This isn't just transportation evolving. It's a complete rewrite of how America might move through its skies. Alef Model A. The Alef Model A is one of the most ambitious entries in the flying car space, blending road driving with vertical takeoff and landing VTOL capability. Built by Alef Aeronautics, it uses an all-electric powertrain with distributed motors hidden inside a mesh-style body, allowing it to drive on normal streets and lift off vertically when needed. Reported specs include a driving range of around 322 km and a flight range close to 177 km with a top aerial speed of roughly 177 km/h. Ground speed is intentionally limited for safety and regulatory compliance. The expected starting price is about $299,999, positioning it as a luxury early adopter vehicle. While still in early testing stages, it represents a serious step toward practical urban air mobility. Archer Midnight. The Archer Midnight is one of the most advanced eVTOL flying car concepts currently moving toward real-world service in the US. Designed by Archer Aviation, it's built for urban air mobility with a focus on short, fast city-to-city routes. The aircraft uses 12 electric motors powered by a 142 kWh battery system delivering a range of around 161 km and a top speed of about 241 km/h. It carries a pilot plus four passengers and is engineered for quick turnaround with charging times close to 10 to 12 minutes. With an expected price of around $5 million, it's aimed at commercial air taxi operators rather than private buyers. Overall, it represents a serious step toward practical electric air travel. Jetpack Aviation Speeder. The Jetpack Aviation Speeder is one of the most extreme takes on personal flight >> [snorts] >> turning the idea of a flying motorcycle into a real engineering project. Developed by Jetpack Aviation, it uses multiple turbine jet engines arranged for VTOL, vertical takeoff and landing. With a top speed of around 240 km/h and a flight ceiling near 15,000 ft. Depending on the version, it offers roughly 10 to 20 minutes of flight time. The ultralight variant is more restricted for basic use, while advanced versions require a pilot's license. Pricing is expected to start around $380,000 placing it firmly in the ultra luxury experimental aircraft category. It feels raw, powerful, and very early stage, but undeniably futuristic in intent. Supernal SA-2. The Supernal SA-2 is a next-generation electric vertical takeoff and landing eVTOL aircraft developed by Hyundai's Supernal division designed to redefine urban air mobility. Built for short city-to-city hops, it features eight distributed electric rotors powered by a 120 kWh battery pack delivering a range of around 97 km and a cruising speed close to 193 km/h. It carries a pilot plus four passengers focusing on quiet, efficient operations with noise levels as low as 45 dB in cruise. The estimated price is around $2.5 million targeting commercial air taxi operators rather than private ownership. Overall, it feels refined, practical, and clearly aimed at scalable urban flight networks. Pivotal Helix. The Pivotal Helix is one of the most accessible personal eVTOL flying cars on the market designed for single pilot recreational flight under FAA Part 103 ultralight rules. Built by Pivotal, it features a lightweight carbon fiber frame, eight electric rotors, and an 8 kWh battery system delivering a range of around 32 km with a cruising speed near 100 km/h. It's engineered for simplicity using joystick controls and automated stability systems to make flight feel almost drone-like. The price starts at about $190,000 placing it in the premium personal aviation category. While its range is limited, it stands out for being license-free, compact, and surprisingly approachable for first-time flyers. J-2000. The Jetoptera J-2000 is one of the most unconventional takes on the flying car concept. Built around its signature fluidic propulsive system that replaces traditional rotors or ducted fans with high-velocity air jets. Designed as a VTOL air taxi, it targets speeds up to 200 knots, around 370 km/h, with a range of roughly 640 km, and a service ceiling near 5,000 m. Powered by a 1,000 to 2,000 horsepower class turboshaft system, and weighing up to 910 kg at max takeoff, it prioritizes efficiency, reduced noise, and simplified mechanics. While the price is not officially confirmed, it's clearly aimed at advanced commercial aviation programs, rather than private ownership. It feels experimental, bold, and genuinely different from typical eVTOL designs. Horizon Cavorite X7. The Horizon Cavorite X7 is one of the [snorts] most ambitious hybrid eVTOL flying car projects developed by Horizon Aircraft in Canada. It uses a unique fan-in-wing system with multiple electric lift fans for vertical takeoff, combined with a turboprop engine for efficient forward cruise. Reported specs include a range of around 800 km, a top speed near 450 km/h, and seating for a pilot plus six passengers, making it closer to a regional air taxi than a personal vehicle. The estimated price is not officially fixed, but is expected to be in the multi-million-dollar range, aimed at commercial operators and defense use. Overall, it feels highly engineered, long-range focused, and more practical than many pure electric eVTOL concepts. AutoFlight Carryall The AutoFlight Carryall is one of the most impressive heavy-lift eVTOL aircraft emerging from China's rapidly advancing low-altitude economy sector. Designed by AutoFlight, it is a fully electric unmanned flying cargo platform built for real-world logistics, not just concept demonstrations. It features a maximum takeoff weight of around 2,000 kg, a payload capacity of up to 400 kg, a cruise speed of about 200 km/h, and a range close to 200 km. The aircraft uses a distributed electric propulsion system with multiple rotors for redundancy and safety. While its official price is not publicly disclosed, it is clearly aimed at commercial operators and government-scale logistics networks. Overall, it feels practical, certified, and far ahead of typical prototype-level flying car concepts. Doroni H1-X The Doroni H1-X is a bold step into personal electric aviation. Designed as a two-seat eVTOL flying car built for everyday mobility rather than pure experimentation. It uses multiple electric lift motors with a ducted fan configuration, delivering a top speed of around 120 mph, a range close to 100 miles, and a cruise speed near 95 mph. Charging is reported to take roughly 20 to 25 minutes under ideal conditions, making it relatively practical for short regional trips. The expected price sits around $300,000 to $400,000, targeting early adopters and aviation enthusiasts. Overall, it feels futuristic yet grounded with real effort toward usability, certification, and real-world adoption, rather than just concept stage hype. Joby S4. The Joby S4 is widely considered one of the most advanced electric air taxis in development today, combining impressive performance with a strong focus on safety and passenger comfort. Built by Joby Aviation, this eVTOL aircraft features six tilting electric propellers and can carry a pilot plus four passengers. It offers a top speed of around 322 km/h and a range of approximately 240 km on a single charge, making it ideal for regional and urban transportation. The expected price is estimated at around $1.3 million to $2 million per aircraft, though it is primarily intended for commercial air taxi fleets. Quiet, efficient, and sleek, the S4 feels like one of the closest concepts to bringing everyday air mobility into reality. Samson Switchblade. The Samson Switchblade is one of the most realistic flying car projects currently nearing production, combining the practicality of a three-wheeled sports car with the freedom of a light aircraft. Powered by a 1.6 L turbocharged engine producing around 190 horsepower, it can reach road speeds of over 200 km/h and cruising flight speeds of approximately 257 km/h. The aircraft offers a flight range of about 725 km, making it suitable for long-distance travel. Its wings and tail retract automatically, allowing it to function as a street-legal vehicle. With an expected base price of around $170,000, the Switchblade stands out as an innovative and surprisingly practical step toward everyday personal aviation. Aska A5. The Aska A5 is one of the most ambitious flying car projects in development, designed to function both as a road legal vehicle and an electric hybrid aircraft. Built to seat four people, it features fold-out wings, six electric propellers, and a range-extending gasoline generator that helps deliver an estimated flight range of around 400 km. In the air, it can reach speeds of approximately 240 km/h while still being capable of driving on public roads. The expected starting price is about $789,000, placing it firmly in the premium aviation category. What makes the A5 stand out is its practicality. It's not just built to fly, but to bridge the gap between driving and personal air travel in a way few competitors attempt. Lift Hexa The Lift Hexa is one of the most approachable personal eVTOL aircraft on the market. Designed to make flying feel as simple as operating a large drone. Built with 18 independent electric motors and propellers, this single-seat aircraft features advanced flight control software that helps keep the experience stable and beginner-friendly. The Hexa offers a top speed of around 100 km/h and a flight time of approximately 15 to 20 minutes per charge, making it ideal for short recreational flights. With a price of about $495,000, it targets adventure seekers and early adopters rather than traditional pilots. What makes the Hexa special is its simplicity. There are no complicated controls, just a futuristic flying experience that feels surprisingly accessible. Opener BlackFly The Opener BlackFly is one of the most unique personal flying vehicles ever developed, blending ultralight aviation with futuristic electric propulsion. Designed as a single-seat eVTOL aircraft, it features eight electric motors, tandem wings, and a lightweight carbon fiber structure. The BlackFly can reach speeds of around 100 km/h and offers a flight range of approximately 40 km under US regulations, with longer ranges possible in other markets. Weighing less than many traditional aircraft, it is designed for simple operation and low maintenance. The estimated price is around $190,000. What makes the BlackFly stand out is its minimalist approach. It's not trying to be a flying car in the traditional sense, but rather a personal aircraft that makes electric flight feel surprisingly attainable and exciting.

Motorcycles are all about freedom, speed, and the thrill of the open road. But, the right gadgets can take that experience to a whole new level. From smart helmets with built-in navigation and communication features to compact tire inflators that fit neatly under your seat, Amazon is packed with innovative gear that feels straight out of the future. In this video, we're counting down 20 of the coolest motorcycle gadgets that can make every ride safer, smarter, and more enjoyable. Whether you spend your time cruising highways, navigating busy city streets, or exploring backroads, there's something here for every rider. From safety-focused upgrades like LED lighting and blind spot mirrors to convenience-enhancing accessories such as wireless charging phone mounts and action cameras, these genius gadgets are designed to transform the way you ride. Bluecap Moto The Bluecap Moto is one of the most innovative motorcycle gadgets we've seen in recent years. These AR-powered navigation sunglasses project turn-by-turn directions, maps, speed alerts, calls, and messages directly into your field of view, helping riders stay focused on the road without constantly checking a phone. Weighing less than 45 g and offering up to 10 hours of battery life, they're designed to fit comfortably under a helmet while maintaining the look of ordinary sunglasses. Features include a micro LED display, Bluetooth connectivity, interchangeable polarized lenses, anti-fog protection, and prescription lens compatibility. The Kickstarter launch price was around $300 with an expected retail price of $400. A truly futuristic upgrade for serious riders. Roame Zeros The Roame Zeros are a clever motorcycle gadget that combines rider safety with everyday style. At first glance, they look like premium high-top riding shoes, but hidden inside are wireless LED turn signals and brake lights that sync directly with your motorcycle through a transmitter module. When you brake or activate your indicators, the LEDs built into the soles light up, making you more visible to surrounding traffic. The shoes also feature abrasion-resistant leather, D3O impact protection in the ankle and toe areas, rechargeable batteries, and a lightweight urban riding design. During the Kickstarter campaign, the Roame Zeros were offered for around $300 with an expected retail price of approximately $400. A truly innovative blend of fashion, technology, and rider safety. Vata 7 The Vata 7 Smart LED Helmet Tech Pack is one of the most ambitious motorcycle safety gadgets on Indiegogo. Designed to make riders far more visible on the road, it features integrated LED lighting that syncs wirelessly with your bike's brake lights and turn signals, ensuring your intentions are clearly communicated to traffic around you. Built with a lightweight carbon fiber shell, it also includes Bluetooth connectivity, anti-fog visor technology, and app-based customization for lighting patterns and alerts. The system is designed to pair within seconds and work across multiple ride types. The campaign pricing started around $599 to $899, depending on configuration, with a projected retail range closer to $899 plus. It's a futuristic concept focused on visibility, safety, and smart rider communication. Iris Helmets The Iris Helmets Smart Dash Cam helmet is an ambitious attempt to merge full riding protection with built-in recording technology. Designed with integrated front and rear HD cameras, it continuously captures your ride to provide clear evidence in case of accidents or disputes. The helmet concept also includes microphone integration, loop recording support, and smartphone connectivity for easy video access and sharing. Built around a safety-focused shell, it aims to remove the need for external helmet-mounted cameras that can feel bulky or unsafe. The Kickstarter pricing for Iris Helmets started around $400 to $600, depending on early pledge tiers, with a projected retail price closer to $700 plus. From revolutionary space technology and advanced robotics to AI-powered home appliances and next-generation gadgets, innovation is transforming the way we live, work, and explore the world around us. In today's video, we're showcasing some of the most exciting breakthroughs designed to make life smarter, easier, and more efficient. Whether it's cutting-edge aerospace engineering, powerful consumer tech, intelligent cleaning solutions, or futuristic mobility systems, these incredible inventions are pushing the boundaries of what's possible. Get ready to discover 18 amazing innovations that are improving everything around you and giving us a glimpse into the future. Let's get started. Dawn Aerospace Mk. II Aurora. The Dawn Aerospace Mk. II Aurora is one of the most exciting aerospace innovations in development today, combining the capabilities of a rocket and an aircraft into a fully reusable suborbital spaceplane. Designed to take off and land on conventional runways, the Aurora can reach altitudes of over 100 km, achieve speeds up to Mach 3.7, and carry payloads of up to 15 kg while offering rapid turnaround times of less than 4 hours between missions. Unlike traditional rockets, it is built for frequent, low-cost operations. While Dawn Aerospace has not publicly disclosed a purchase price, earlier estimates suggested mission costs around $50,000 per flight, making space access more affordable for research and commercial applications. Axiom LCVG The Axiom LCVG liquid cooling and ventilation garment is a remarkable innovation developed by Axiom Space and Prada for NASA's upcoming Artemis lunar missions. Worn beneath the Axiom U spacesuit, this advanced garment keeps astronauts comfortable during long moonwalks by circulating cooled water through integrated tubes while delivering fresh oxygen and removing carbon dioxide. Its fully redundant cooling system provides added safety, a major improvement over previous designs. Built for up to 8-hour spacewalks, the LCVG combines cutting-edge aerospace engineering with advanced materials technology. Since it is a specialized spaceflight system, no public retail price has been announced. Majorana 2. Majorana 2 is Microsoft's breakthrough in topological quantum computing, designed to push the limits of stability and scalability in next-generation processors. Its key innovation lies in significantly improved qubit reliability, with quantum states lasting around 20 seconds and reaching up to a minute, about 1,000 times better than previous designs. Built using an advanced material stack including lead-based superconductors, it enhances resistance to environmental noise and cosmic interference. The chip is still in early-stage research, so no commercial price is available. Microsoft positions it as a foundational step towards scalable, fault-tolerant quantum computers by 2029 with applications in science and complex problem-solving. Schiebel Camcopter S-100. The Schiebel Camcopter S-100 is a highly advanced rotary-wing unmanned aerial system designed for intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance missions across land and maritime environments. Its key innovation lies in its fully autonomous vertical takeoff and landing capability combined with long-range endurance of up to 10 hours and a mission range of around 200 km. The system supports multiple payloads including electro-optical, infrared, radar, and communication sensors, making it highly versatile in defense and search operations.
